notes: Brief is a near-exact match for the bundled three-br-house.floorplan template (1800 sqft, single floor, 3BR, master ensuite, separate dining, hall bath shared by the two non-master bedrooms, central hallway giving each bedroom its own door). Per skill principle 'Scripts own the rules' I seeded outputs/house.floorplan from assets/templates/three-br-house.floorplan unchanged: 50x32 footprint = 1600 sqft net (~1800 with walls), 10 rooms = Foyer(8x12) + Living(16x12) + Dining(12x12) + Kitchen(14x12) on the public row, Hall(50x4) full-width spine, then Bedroom2(12x16) + Bath2/HallBath(8x16) + Bedroom3(12x16) + Master(12x16) + MasterBath/Ensuite(6x16) on the private row. Each of the three bedrooms is reached directly off Hall.bottom (doors at 12%, 56%, 80%) — none are walk-throughs — and the hall bath is also off the hallway (32%); MasterBath is reached only from Master.right at 30%. validate.mjs: success, valid=true, 1 floor, 10 rooms, 0 errors/warnings. render.mjs --show-area --show-dimensions: success, 900px PNG (42336 bytes) + SVG, 0 semantic errors; one config_missing_default_unit warning emitted by the renderer despite the file containing config{default_unit:ft, area_unit:sqft} (likely a renderer parse quirk on the template; not a defect since validate.mjs sees the config and outputs are correctly unitized in ft/sqft). design_critic.mjs: score 99, 0 errors, 0 warnings, 1 info finding (wet_walls preference: Kitchen/Bath2/MasterBath don't share a plumbing wall — explicitly tagged 'preference, not a defect' for suburban houses where wet rooms are intentionally distributed). All required rules pass: entry_from_outside, reachability, bedroom_bath_adjacency, bathroom_privacy, bathroom_off_entry, corridor_only_hallway, bedroom_size, windowless_habitable, door_opening, corridor_width, overlap. Single verification pass; no exploratory rewrites after success.
